rulings.Case Title: CHERYL LEA HOGAN VS. MADELINE ZIMIN MAO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: (Amended) Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default And Vacate Default Judgment, For Return Of Wrongfully Seized Funds, Request For Monetary Sanctions And Request For Leave To File Proposed Answer And X-Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, March 5, 2010, line 3, DEFENDANT MADELINE MAO (Amended) Motion to Set Aside Default and Vacate Default Judgment, for Return of Wrongfully Seized Funds, Request for Monetary Sanctions and Request for Leave to File Proposed Answer and Cross-Complaint. Motion granted. Defendant lacked actual notice of the summons and complaint, which was not due to avoidance of service or inexcusable neglect. Requests for attorney fees and sanctions denied. 2/5/2010 ruling vacated as improvidently heard. Request to order bond to be posted denied. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: MARGARET BARROWS VS. WELLS FARGO BANK N.A. et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, March 5, 2010, line 5, DEFENDANT ADDUS HEALTHCARE INC. MOTION TO STRIKE. Motion to strike off calendar as moot in view of ruling on demurrer. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: MARGARET BARROWS VS. WELLS FARGO BANK N.A. et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, March 5, 2010, line 5, DEFENDANT ADDUS HEALTHCARE INC. AMENDED DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend as to 1st, and 2nd causes of action. Plaintiff must plead facts showing Addus took or assisted in the taking, or knew about the conduct by others. Plaintiff to allege specific facts regarding Addus's duties and requirements, and support allegations of recklessness. Government Code section 15610 et. seq. require affirmative knowledge or conduct. Demurrer overruled as to 3rd cause of action. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: JOANDA, LLC, A NEVADA LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY VS. 246 SECOND STREET OWNERS ASSOCIATION, A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, March 5, 2010, line 7, DEFENDANTS ALAN KO, TOM YAMAMOTO, BILL BILLINGHAM, ROBERT LEE, 246 SECOND STREET OWNERS ASSOCIATION, ARMAND HACOBIAN DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Demurrer overruled, ten days leave to answer. Complaint is filed within the applicable four year statute of limitations. Cutujian vs. Benedict Hills Estates Assoc. (1996) 41 Cal.App. 4th 1379, 1384-1385. Complaint sufficiently pled to survive a demurrer. Section 4.7 of the CC&Rs discusses water leaks that damage another Unit and cooperation with the Association in the inspection and correction of the problem. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: RETIREMENT BOARD OF THE SAN FRANCISCO CITY AND VS. AMERICAN ARBITRATION ASSOCIATION
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Petition And Petition For Peremptory Writ Of Mandate
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, March 5, 2010, line 8, PETITIONER RETIREMENT BOARD OF THE SAN FRANCISCO CITY AND COUNTY Petition For Peremptory Writ Of Mandate. Petition denied. The standard of review is independent judgment. In exercising its independent judgment, a trial court must afford a strong presumption of correctness concerning the administartive findings, and the party challenging the administrative decision bears the burden of convincing the court that the administrative findings are contrary to the weight of the evidence. Fukuda v. City of Angels (1999) 20 Cal. 4th 805, 817. Although Dr. Renbaum, the agreed medical examiner, stated Propst is capable of continuing his regular desk job, he placed work restrictions on Propst that included preclusion from heavy lifting, repetitive use of the non-dominant left extremity at or about shoulder level, repetitive forceful gripping, grasping, pushing, pulling or pinching with the left non-dominant extremity at or about shoulder level. These preclusions cannot be totally excluded from Propst's "regular job" if returned to work. Dr. Prieto, Propst's treating physician, documents continuing problems with the left shoulder. Although Propst stated at RT 19 that he could perform his full duties and retired because of his age, he described his physical limitations and difficulties that would prevent him from performing his duties. RT 15-18. In view of all of the evidence petitioner has failed to establish that the hearing officer erred. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: CARL ERNST JR VS. HOLEY VENTURES, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, UNLAWFUL DETAINER
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, March 5, 2010, line 10, PLAINTIFF CARL ERNST MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Motion for summary judgment denied, triable issues of fact raised. Affirmative defenses sufficiently raised in answer. Triable issue of fact whether plaintiff assumed the license by demanding and accepting rent after the effective date of the lease rejection in bankruptcy court. Triable issue of fact whether plaintiff created a new month-to-month tenancy by accepting rent, and the new tenancy cannot be terminated by a 3-day notice. CC section 1946. Summary adjudication denied, court cannot adjudicate factual issues. CCP section 437c(f)(1). =(302/CWW)
